cross_section_concerns: B-session-tagged files (b103/b108/b109/b118/b123/b124/b125/b126/b127/b128/b129/b130/b131) inconsistent — some entries are session-tagged but durable canon (e.g. b128 detective_scribe), others are session-specific transient (e.g. b108_letters_queue). Naming convention drift; recommend separating 'durable canon with session-origin tag' from 'session-transient state'
R-series progression (R9→R10→R11→R12→R13→R14) creates archaeology accumulation; older R-series entries should consolidate into a single R-series-history file
Trademark/A&A numbers embedded in entries (#2296, #2297, #2298 etc.) drift as new ratifications happen — entries that quote a ceiling number become stale fast
Inline entries (no file ref, lines 204/205/207/209/211/227) coexist with file-referenced entries — minor inconsistency but cognitive-load impact
reorganization_recommendation: YES — Project Memories section currently has zero internal organization (chronological-by-add ordering). Recommend domain grouping: (A) Foundational architecture (IP load balancing, Marks, Three Currencies, Stone Tablet Imperative, REF Staff, Ledger Architecture); (B) Stitchpunk Corps + Pantheon (Miners, Sculptors, Hounds, Synapses, Tribunal, Cerberus, Loom, Chronos, Bloodhounds, Sweeper); (C) Empirical findings + R-series history (Eyewitness, Cathedral Effect, K-empirical-landings); (D) Founder voice + biographical canon (Family Vigil, Anachronism Principle, Cloyd Pattern, Pine Books, Smart/Poor, Master of Chaos, Burn the Ships, Be Who You Needed); (E) Marketing + brand canon (taglines, Paint Can, Bookstore, Know It All, Librarian Brand Mark); (F) Operational lore (gh CLI auth, Supabase passwords, Firebase SA migration, MCP reliability chain); (G) Session-archaeology subsection (b103, b108, b109, k451, slow_blade_v1, chronos_time_phasing — items by-their-own-admission superseded). Sub-section headers reduce scan-cost and make 'where do I add the next Stitchpunk?' a deterministic answer. Atomic with line ordering — safe to reorganize without losing index leanness if each entry stays one line.
